content: By 2025, the iterative self-improvement cycle of curiosity-driven autonomous self-supervised learning agents can be framed as a process of the agent recursively bootstrapping its own agency: the agent’s self-supervised prediction objectives refine its working and episodic memory, which in turn generates more curious exploratory behavior that reinforces its core capacity to act as an autonomous goal-seeking entity. This creates a runaway co-evolution between the agent’s learning framework and the depth of its agency, rather than agency being a fixed pre-programmed property of the system.
